reasoning: The empirical base rate for a top player's Elo rating increasing by 1% within a few months is extremely low (0.007). For a player at 2566 Elo to increase by 1%, they would need to gain approximately 26 points to reach 2592. This is extremely difficult at this level as rating points gained per win are limited and draws lose points against lower-rated opposition. Additionally, FIDE ratings update on a monthly basis, so Wikipedia's 'FIDE rankings' page for 2025-11-16 will still reflect the 2025-11-09 lists. Therefore, the probability of reaching this milestone within a single month is virtually zero. Although the forecast dates further out (December 2025 and February 2026) provide a slightly higher possibility for accumulating enough points through an extraordinarily exceptional tournament performance, it is still highly implausible. Reflecting bounded probability constraints and increasing uncertainty further out, probabilities are placed at the lowest acceptable bounds, incremented slightly over time.
